I'll now create `src/components/WorkspaceList.ts` to manage workspace viewing, creation, and deletion, including an error-handling mechanism and a refresh feature.
I'll now create `src/components/ChannelList.ts` to manage channel viewing, creation, and deletion within the currently open workspace, incorporating a refresh button and error handling.
I'll now create `src/components/PostView.ts`, which will manage the post display area, subscribe to real-time updates from OwlDB, and organize posts into a hierarchical tree structure for rendering.

I'll now create `src/components/PostItem.ts` to render individual posts, including author information, formatted content, reactions, and a reply button. I'll also implement a basic markdown parser for italics, bolding, and links.
I'll now create `src/components/PostInput.ts` to provide a robust post creation and reply interface, including formatting tools, emoji insertion, and specific behavior for newlines versus submission.
I'll now update `src/main.ts` to initialize the `AppRoot` component and append it to the document body, effectively starting the application.

blocks.text: I have completed the assignment, implementing a full-featured messaging application using Web Components and TypeScript. Key features include a mandatory login modal with Bearer token authentication, workspace and channel management (creation, deletion, and selection), hierarchical and real-time post display via Server-Sent Events (SSE), markdown support for message formatting, and a reaction system. Additionally, I implemented a "Workspace Description" extension and provided the required Cypress custom commands for automated testing. All data received from OwlDB is validated using AJV and JSON schemas.
